fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Pennsylvania Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Pennsylvania — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license pennsylvania, dental assistant certification pennsylvania, RDA pennsylvania, DANB CDA pennsylvania, dental assistant radiography pennsylvania

license_types.title: Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA)
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Pennsylvania.

steps.title: Apply for State Credential
steps.description: Submit application to the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry with required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry by certification.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pennsylvania requires Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Pennsylvania.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Function Dental Assistant (EFDA) through the Pennsylvania State Board of Dentistry by certification.
